humanities_role_explanation: Uses Christian religious art images as the domain content and is designed specifically for art-historical iconographic understanding and classification
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Multi-modal evaluation combining Christian iconographic artwork images with natural language prompts for classification tasks, including class labels, detailed descriptions, and few-shot examples
analytical_tasks_explanation: Single-label classification of Christian saints and religious figures in artwork, requiring semantic understanding of iconographic elements, symbols, and religious themes. Tasks evaluate factual recognition of specific saints, contextual interpretation using Iconclass descriptions, and adaptation through few-shot exemplars

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Evaluates multiple general-purpose multimodal models including CLIP, SigLIP, GPT-4o, and Gemini across standardized classification tasks

evaluates_generative_llm.note: GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, Gemini 2.5 Pro, Gemini 2.5 Flash

llm_is_primary_system.note: LLMs and VLMs are the primary systems being benchmarked for iconographic classification performance

arts_humanities_data.note: Visual Arts and Performing Arts - focuses on Christian religious paintings and iconographic classification using art historical datasets

quantitative_metrics.note: Accuracy scores, consistency percentages across datasets and models

english_language.note: Paper written in English with English-language benchmark

summary: This study benchmarks Vision-Language Models and Multimodal Large Language Models for classifying Christian iconography in artwork using three datasets (ArtDL, ICONCLASS, Wikidata) across zero-shot and few-shot scenarios. The research evaluates how models perform on single-label classification of Christian saints and religious figures, comparing performance with different prompt enrichment strategies.
key_findings: Gemini-2.5 Pro achieved highest accuracy of 90.45% on ArtDL, outperforming ResNet50 baselines. Performance varied significantly by dataset, with Wikidata showing lower accuracies (best: 64.86% by SigLIP). Enriching prompts with Iconclass descriptions generally improved zero-shot performance, while few-shot learning produced mixed results with mostly lower accuracy.
